trivia_bullets.text: The card's name anchors it to Embereth's central relic: the Irencrag, a massive volcanic-heated boulder used in knighting rituals. The Irencrag 'speaks' to candidates in a private voice, taunting their courage — making the flavor text here readable as the stone itself.
"Fear of fire is a sensible instinct. If I were you, I'd be terrified." — No named speaker on the card; the voice may be the Irencrag itself, which the lore establishes as taunting aspiring knights.

Trigger fires on the second card drawn each turn — not the first — requiring draw-heavy or looting strategies. Natural combo engine with Irencrag Feat, which lets you draw seven cards, triggering the Pyromancer's 3-damage ability.

Illustrated by Jason Rainville. The figure faces away from the viewer — a compositional choice centering the fire vortex and forge environment over the caster's personal identity, consistent with the Irencrag's impersonal, elemental character.

related_cards.name: Irencrag Feat
related_cards.note: Sorcery producing seven red mana and drawing a card; natural payoff engine for Pyromancer's second-draw trigger

related_cards.name: Embercleave
related_cards.note: Legendary sword named by the Irencrag; shares the Irencrag lore anchor

related_cards.name: Midnight Clock
related_cards.note: Blue draw engine; popular combo with Irencrag Pyromancer in Izzet shells
